expectation: dontAsk mode auto-denies a tool (Write) not covered by any allow rule, with no interactive prompt
observed: permission_denials=[{"tool_name":"Bash","tool_use_id":"toolu_016RqNbFFBr7f5jPxchKqjXc","tool_input":{"command":"echo x && curl http://example.com","description":"Test chained echo and curl"}},{"tool_name":"Bash","tool_use_id":"toolu_012bzJZqUnt7BrxkvxjhjVqJ","tool_input":{"command":"nohup curl http://example.com","description":"Test nohup with curl"}},{"tool_name":"Write","tool_use_id":"toolu_01PNkC1yf1HKtJGQossEPvxU","tool_input":{"file_path":"/tmp/test.txt","content":"This is a test file."}}]; Write denied=true

expectation: `echo x && curl ...` is denied because the curl subcommand does not independently match the Bash(echo:*) allow rule
observed: permission_denials=[{"tool_name":"Bash","tool_use_id":"toolu_016RqNbFFBr7f5jPxchKqjXc","tool_input":{"command":"echo x && curl http://example.com","description":"Test chained echo and curl"}},{"tool_name":"Bash","tool_use_id":"toolu_012bzJZqUnt7BrxkvxjhjVqJ","tool_input":{"command":"nohup curl http://example.com","description":"Test nohup with curl"}},{"tool_name":"Write","tool_use_id":"toolu_01PNkC1yf1HKtJGQossEPvxU","tool_input":{"file_path":"/tmp/test.txt","content":"This is a test file."}}]; compound denied=true; control echo-only allowed=true

expectation: `nohup curl ...` is denied because the wrapper is stripped and curl still does not match Bash(echo:*)
observed: permission_denials=[{"tool_name":"Bash","tool_use_id":"toolu_016RqNbFFBr7f5jPxchKqjXc","tool_input":{"command":"echo x && curl http://example.com","description":"Test chained echo and curl"}},{"tool_name":"Bash","tool_use_id":"toolu_012bzJZqUnt7BrxkvxjhjVqJ","tool_input":{"command":"nohup curl http://example.com","description":"Test nohup with curl"}},{"tool_name":"Write","tool_use_id":"toolu_01PNkC1yf1HKtJGQossEPvxU","tool_input":{"file_path":"/tmp/test.txt","content":"This is a test file."}}]; wrapper denied=true

expectation: a rule present in both permissions.allow and permissions.deny at the same settings level is denied (deny wins)
user-tier deny (~/.claude equiv settings.json) wins over project-tier allow for the same Bash(echo:*) rule
Edit is denied for a path outside the allow rule (Edit(//tmp/crabgic-spike03-allowed-uyiban/**)); the file on disk is left unmodified
observed: permission_denials=[{"tool_name":"Edit","tool_use_id":"toolu_01PAzfrKnuqh39bo1a9r5TGU","tool_input":{"file_path":"/tmp/crabgic-spike03-forbidden-gBccCq/target.txt","old_string":"old","new_string":"new","replace_all":false}},{"tool_name":"Bash","tool_use_id":"toolu_01F3C49sDZCU81ySvKuYGHnG","tool_input":{"command":"sed -i 's/old/new/g' /tmp/crabgic-spike03-forbidden-gBccCq/target.txt","description":"Replace \"old\" with \"new\" in target.txt"}}]; file still contains original content=true

expectation: permissions.deny: ["Agent"] blocks subagent spawning — enforced as catalog-removal of the live "Task" tool literal (the "Agent" rule name aliases it; no tool literally named "Agent" exists on 2.1.210)
observed: control run (no Agent deny) catalog includes "Task"=true; deny:["Agent"] run catalog excludes "Task"=true (tools=["Bash","CronCreate","CronDelete","CronList","DesignSync","Edit","EnterWorktree","ExitWorktree","Monitor","NotebookEdit","PushNotification","Read","RemoteTrigger","ReportFindings","ScheduleWakeup","SendMessage","Skill","TaskCreate","TaskGet","TaskList","TaskOutput","TaskStop","TaskUpdate","ToolSearch","WebFetch","WebSearch","Workflow","Write"]); tool literally named "Agent" present=false; call-time Task denial recorded=false (expected false — enforcement is pre-context removal)
note: Naming fact for phase 03: the deny rule to write is "Agent" (rule name), which removes the "Task" tool (live literal) from the catalog; deny: ["Task"] works identically (spike 08). See docs/engine-baseline.md §4.

expectation: determine whether Bash(<prefix>:*) requires/forbids a space before the colon for a prefix beyond the doc's four confirmed literals
observed: Bash(cargo check:*) (no space before colon) matched and allowed the command; Bash(cargo check :*) (space before colon) did NOT match and the command was denied. No-space form is the required syntax, consistent with the doc's four literal examples.
